Italy Travel Guide — Rome, Florence, and Venice: This classic "Grand Tour" itinerary covers ten days across three iconic Italian cities, best experienced in spring (April–May) or early fall (September–October) to avoid peak summer crowds and heat. In Rome, prioritize the Colosseum and Roman Forum, the Vatican Museums, and an evening stroll through Trastevere; book timed entry tickets for the Colosseum well in advance. Florence in two days should include the Uffizi Gallery, Ponte Vecchio, and a hike to Piazzale Michelangelo at sunset. Venice is best explored on foot and by vaporetto, lingering in the quiet Dorsoduro and Cannaregio neighborhoods beyond the tourist hotspots.
